source_or_gap: Corben-authored semantic-constitution proposal: stable Form lineages, immutable semantic versions, Essence Contracts, six planes, branch-protected dynamics, qualified grounding, proof-carrying commitments, semantic transactions, packet compilation, and federation.
chapter_action_policy: Strengthen the nineteen existing owners, with Claim Ledgers as the epistemic primary owner, Security and Provenance as protected lifecycle owners, and Integrated Reference Architecture as the six-plane join owner. Do not add a PWM-shaped chapter unless a working trusted semantic kernel establishes a distinct public interface and evidence surface.
merge_or_split_policy: Merge epistemic objects into Claim Ledgers, bounded packets into Virtual Context ABI, branch semantics into Context Transactions and Planning, and governance or federation into their existing owners. Split only after implementation reveals an otherwise unowned semantic-kernel boundary.
boundary_decision_rationale: The six planes are a cross-layer constitution around existing stack components. Treating them as one chapter would duplicate ontology, context, planning, evidence, authority, learning, federation, and integration responsibilities.
external_literature_need: passage-review the paper's cited primary ontology, provenance, context, conceptual-space, concept-model, causal-representation, world-model, ontology-evolution, mapping, and proof-carrying sources before external-support use
add current independent semantic-drift, knowledge-graph evolution, and world-model comparators
verify all 2026 standards and publication-status metadata from primary sources
proof_or_test_backlog: freeze a minimal semantic kernel and schemas for Form family/version, Essence Contract, context, proposition, attestation, commitment, proof, branch, grounding contract, and semantic transaction
implement identity/version, claim-ledger, proof/governance, grounded-world-model, and adaptive-federation profiles incrementally
run longitudinal semantic-drift tests across model, ontology, policy, and grounding replacement
test context leakage, branch escape, authority escalation, evidence laundering, mapping abuse, and dependency-index evasion
compare full PWM and component ablations against strong retrieval, vector-memory, graph, ontology/provenance, context-aware, latent-world-model, and concept-model baselines
measure semantic continuity, planning value, patch safety, packet sufficiency, information-flow leakage, latency, storage, review burden, and maintenance cost together
retain partial-profile wins and reject components that do not beat simpler baselines
evidence_transition_precondition: longitudinal held-out benchmark with hidden adversarial changes
independent evaluator and strong baseline tuning
transfer across at least formal, control, and institutional domains for broad claims
promotion_blockers: no working semantic kernel or PWM substrate
residuals: governance capture and total maintenance cost
next_action: Build a bounded identity-plus-claim-ledger profile only after freezing strong baselines, longitudinal semantic mutations, branch and authority attacks, independent adjudication, and total-cost accounting; expand profiles only when each earns measured value.
non_claims: does not solve grounding or establish one correct ontology
does not establish safety, efficiency, novelty, SOTA, AGI, or ASI
does not publish the raw source
